pattern: For the middle index of a triplet, only the single largest value before it and the single largest value after it can ever matter, so tracking a running best-so-far on each side turns every triplet comparison into a handful of numbers instead of a search.
hints: For a fixed middle index, the first slot of the triplet only ever wants to be as large as possible, and the third slot only ever wants to be as large as possible -- nothing else about the earlier or later elements changes the outcome.

Sweep through candidate middle positions while keeping the largest value seen among everything before it, and separately know the largest value among everything after it, so each middle position can be scored in constant time.

Score every possible middle position with (best-value-before minus that position's own value) times best-value-after, keep a running maximum across all of them, and clamp the final answer up to zero if nothing beat it.

edge_inputs.description: minimal length, strictly increasing so the only triplet is negative
minimal length arranged so the only triplet is positive
every value identical, so every triplet scores exactly zero
maximum length, every value pinned to the maximum allowed
a deep dip flanked by two equally huge values
strictly decreasing values, where a positive triplet still exists
a small dip immediately followed by one huge value
a small irregular sequence with no special structure

stress.description: random arrays at the maximum allowed length
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(3, min(size, 100))
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 1000000) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]

stress.description: arrays drawn from a small palette of values so ties and repeats are common
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(3, min(size, 100))
    palette = [rng.randint(1, 1000000) for _ in range(4)]
    nums = [rng.choice(palette) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]
